note: ckeditor5_template ships a single configurable CKEditor 5 plugin (Drupal plugin id ckeditor5_template_template, toolbar item `template`) and has no admin form, no configure route, no permissions and no Drush commands: everything lives on the per-format editor config entity under settings.plugins.ckeditor5_template_template + settings.toolbar.items. Easy cases are response-graded. Medium cases pre-configure a known format via `setup` and ask the agent to read live editor config back. Hard cases clear state via `reset`, have the agent add + configure the Template button on a CKEditor 5 format, and `verify` the editor config entity (exit 0 = pass).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site using the contrib `ckeditor5_template` module, what is the Drupal CKEditor 5 plugin id it contributes, and what is the toolbar item id you drag onto a text format's CKEditor 5 toolbar to enable it?
evals.expected_output: The Drupal CKEditor 5 plugin id is `ckeditor5_template_template` (JS plugin `template.Template`); the toolbar item id is `template`.

evals.prompt: How do you enable the CKEditor 5 Template button, and is there a site-wide settings form? Explain where in Drupal you turn it on and where its settings (the template file, the button label) are stored.
evals.expected_output: There is no site-wide settings form and no `configure` route. You enable it per text format at /admin/config/content/formats by dragging the Template button into the active CKEditor 5 toolbar; settings live on the format's editor config entity under settings.plugins.ckeditor5_template_template (file_path, custom_toolbar_text, show_toolbar_text) with the `template` item in settings.toolbar.items.

evals.prompt: For the `ckeditor5_template` CKEditor 5 plugin, which configuration keys does each text format store, and which one points at the JSON file that lists the available templates? What fields does each entry in that JSON file have?
evals.expected_output: The plugin config keys are file_path (path to the templates JSON file, from Drupal root), custom_toolbar_text (button label) and show_toolbar_text (0/1). Each entry in the JSON array has title, icon (inline SVG), description and html (the markup inserted at the cursor).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, one text format has the CKEditor 5 Template button (the ckeditor5_template plugin) enabled on its toolbar. Inspect the live editor configuration and tell me: which text format is it, and what template JSON file (the file_path) is configured for it?
evals.expected_output: The Full HTML (full_html) format has the `template` toolbar item enabled, configured with file_path /modules/contrib/ckeditor5_template/template/ckeditor5_template.json.example (read from editor.editor.full_html settings.plugins.ckeditor5_template_template).

evals.prompt: A text format on this site has the ckeditor5_template Template button enabled and it shows a custom text label next to the icon in the CKEditor 5 toolbar. Inspect the live editor config and tell me which format it is and what label text (custom_toolbar_text) editors see.
evals.expected_output: The Basic HTML (basic_html) format has the template button with show_toolbar_text = 1 and custom_toolbar_text = "Reusable snippets" (from editor.editor.basic_html settings.plugins.ckeditor5_template_template).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, add the CKEditor 5 Template button (from the ckeditor5_template module) to the Full HTML text format's CKEditor 5 toolbar and configure it to use the module's bundled example templates file. Apply it to the live site so the `template` toolbar item and the ckeditor5_template_template plugin configuration (with a valid file_path) exist on the full_html editor config entity.
evals.expected_output: editor.editor.full_html now has `template` in settings.toolbar.items and settings.plugins.ckeditor5_template_template with a non-empty file_path that resolves to a real file on disk (e.g. /modules/contrib/ckeditor5_template/template/ckeditor5_template.json.example).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, enable the ckeditor5_template Template button on the Basic HTML text format's CKEditor 5 toolbar and point it at a templates JSON file that exists on the site. Save it live so the `template` toolbar item and the ckeditor5_template_template plugin config (with a valid file_path) are present on the basic_html editor config entity.
evals.expected_output: editor.editor.basic_html now has `template` in settings.toolbar.items and settings.plugins.ckeditor5_template_template with a non-empty file_path resolving to a real file on disk.
